title: podman 4.1.1: 'too many links' error when podman pulling some images to FUSE Gluster imagestore rootdir (error pulling to NFSv3 as well)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

podman 4.1.1: 'too many links' error when podman pulling some images to FUSE-based ~or NFS-based~ imagestore rootdir

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
podman  --root /mnt/glusterclient/imagestore pull --creds 'xxx:yyy' some.registry/myimage:version
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Trying to pull ...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 5429737859dd done  
Copying blob c78ee5f04f57 done  
Copying blob 4592cb53756e done  
Copying blob 57d28b517b78 done

Copying blob b643f81a3624 done  
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4592cb53756e5e1ecf048d6f0603ceb7ac3e25c371847cd6f5aee28dd279c7ec": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): link /var/lib/yum/yumdb/a/89a72f85b51883b1cf79084106f2ef5b96c710dc-audit-libs-2.8.5-4.el7-x86_64/checksum_type /var/lib/yum/yumdb/l/f32eacc0ec9629ff060a9fc323e2ee070a5cbf35-libuuid-2.23.2-65.0.1.el7_9.1-x86_64/checksum_type: too many links
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Debug output error:

```
DEBU[0107] Error pulling candidate ...: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4592cb53756e5e1ecf048d6f0603ceb7ac3e25c371847cd6f5aee28dd279c7ec": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): link /var/lib/yum/yumdb/a/89a72f85b51883b1cf79084106f2ef5b96c710dc-audit-libs-2.8.5-4.el7-x86_64/checksum_type /var/lib/yum/yumdb/l/f32eacc0ec9629ff060a9fc323e2ee070a5cbf35-libuuid-2.23.2-65.0.1.el7_9.1-x86_64/checksum_type: too many links 
Error: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4592cb53756e5e1ecf048d6f0603ceb7ac3e25c371847cd6f5aee28dd279c7ec": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): link /var/lib/yum/yumdb/a/89a72f85b51883b1cf79084106f2ef5b96c710dc-audit-libs-2.8.5-4.el7-x86_64/checksum_type /var/lib/yum/yumdb/l/f32eacc0ec9629ff060a9fc323e2ee070a5cbf35-libuuid-2.23.2-65.0.1.el7_9.1-x86_64/checksum_type: too many links
```

~~I see the same issue when using NFS.~~  Strangely, this only occurs for certain images.  In our case it's an internal image created via docker.

This works properly when I pull to a local directory.   I am trying to use the additionalimagestores feature, which is why I have run into this issue.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.1
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: @rhatdan @giuseppe PTAL

Apologies; I though NFS had the same error but it is slightly different.

NFS error:
```
DEBU[0104] Error pulling candidate ...: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:c78ee5f04f57a33601b4cf67b41050779bb5ebee814cabc8c8237a93b8535fcf": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not supported

```

I can repro this issue using the public image oraclelinux:7 -- pulling to a gluster mountpoint.

I think the error is coming directly from the file system so there is not much Podman can do.  What is the underlying FUSE file system?

I'm using a gluster mountpoint in this case, on top of an XFS filesystem

```
mount -t glusterfs ...:/gv0 /mnt/glusterclient

# rpm -qa|grep -i gluster|grep fuse
glusterfs-fuse-8.6-2.module+el8.5.0+20523+1496d785.x86_64

# df -khl /mnt
Filesystem                  Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/mapper/ocivolume-root  189G   14G  176G   8% /

# blkid|grep ocivolume-root
/dev/mapper/ocivolume-root: UUID="cc7fcbb1-bc82-4e5a-9fdc-3767d5c32c48" BLOCK_SIZE="4096" TYPE="xfs"
```
(I'll create a separate issue for not being able to pull to a NFS imagestore directory, as the error is slightly different)

NFS Shares currently can not store images, since they will not allow multiple UIDs to be written to them  The server does not understand User Namespace so the server sees the Podman user attempting to chown a file to a different UID and blocks it.  Similarly most other network shares would block this kind of access as well.
Re: pulling to NFS, I noticed some blog info along those lines, but I was hoping that I might be able to chmod -R to get around that issue.   Is there any workaround?

@giuseppe can we mount a fuse-overlayfs system on top?

@coco80021 you can ssh to the server and then able to share from there.

Related -- do you know of any additionalimagestore volume types which are more likely to work during the pulling stage?  So far, I've tried NFS and gluster, but no luck on either.  I'm looking for other ideas.  (ceph? intranet bittorrent? etc)

I believe there are two-parts to this:
a) copying the image to the imagestore shared location.  For now, I can do this via workaround by pulling to a local rootdir, then manually copying that rootdir to the shared location using cp -pr.   So I can get the files there, though it is 2 hops.
b) podman/cri-o pulling from the imagestore shared location.   I have been able to get this to work properly with Gluster.  Still testing NFS.

So (b) works with Gluster and (a) functions with either Gluster or NFS with a workaround.

My goal is to avoid the manual copy in (a) from local-dir to shared-dir, if that is possible. (not sure)

>>>
NFS Shares currently can not store images, since they will not allow multiple UIDs to be written to them The server does not understand User Namespace so the server sees the Podman user attempting to chown a file to a different UID and blocks it. Similarly most other network shares would block this kind of access as well.

The reason I ask is because it works properly for me to pull to a local XFS imagestore directory, then cp -pr to either NFS or Gluster.  But it fails when I pull directly to NFS or Gluster imagestore dir.  

I am curious why this is.   I am running as root, and I can chown Gluster and NFS files.  I can also set them large and otherwise unused userID/groupID values.

It seems podman (running as root) can't do what I can do as a root user on the same store, using cp -pr <localImageStoreDir> <glusterOrNFSImageStoreDir>
Are you running podman as root or in rootless mode?
I am running podman as root.

I just noticed that for some images, such as container-registry.oracle.com/os/oraclelinux:7, the image pull works with no issue.  So it seems to be image-specific, though it's unclear what the image issue is.

I will work to identify what line(s) in the Dockerfile are causing this issue.
Could it be that images with setfilecaps or something like that is not allowed on an NFS share.  I would think this is something to do with Xattrs of File Capabilities.

(NFS) Thanks for the pointer.  I can confirm that is an Xattr issue when pulling to our NFSv3 imagestore.

DEBU[0031] Error pulling candidate someregistry/someimage:someversion: writing blob: adding layer with blob "sha256:4592cb53756e5e1ecf048d6f0603ceb7ac3e25c371847cd6f5aee28dd279c7ec": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): lsetxattr /: operation not supported 
Error: Error processing tar file(exit status 1): lsetxattr /: operation not supported

I'm going to try to build fuse_xattrs and get that setup on top of NFS v3, to see if that will let me get further.   Unfortunately, our NAS does not support NFS v4.

I think this issue can be closed, as it appears there is no underlying podman or containers/storage issue here.  Thanks for the assistance in identifying the issues.

Summary (from my perspective) in case anyone else runs into the same issue(s):

For podman pulling (as root) to Gluster imagestore: the 'too many links' error is a Gluster error.   I see the # of hard links is configurable in Gluster, so I will investigate along that path.

For podman pulling (as root) to NFS imagestore: the 'operation not supported' errors are because it is a NFS v3 NAS server without support for extended attributes.   The fix here would be to move to a NFS v4 server.
After setting the gluster hardlinks limit to 0, I was able to successfully pull images using podman to the gluster volume mountpoint.
